data.title: SM Energy prices $1B senior notes offering due 2034
data.description:
• SM Energy Company (SM) priced an upsized $1B offering of 6.625% senior notes due 2034.

  • The notes will be issued at par and are expected to close on March 9, 2026.

  • Net proceeds will help fund a previously announced cash tender offer of up to $750M for its 8.375% senior notes due 2028.

  • The company may also use proceeds for general corporate purposes, including additional repayment of its 2028 notes.

data.title: Gold ticks higher as Middle East war breaks Dubai's gold pipeline
data.description:
Gold futures edged higher Wednesday, with flights out of gold hub Dubai mostly canceled due to the fighting in the Middle East, leaving traders unable to move their metal.

The war has grounded flights across the Persian Gulf region, shutting down the United Arab Emirates' role as the critical link between African mines, European refiners, and Asian buyers - creating a supply bottleneck that traders say could trigger sharp regional price dislocations in the days ahead.

Dubai handles ~20% of the world's gold flows, and because of how much gold moves through the country, some analysts think gold prices may start to reflect increased costs.

"Material can of course be sourced from elsewhere, but I would expect that to come at a price as we would be looking at a seller's market," Rhona O’Connell of StoneX said in a note.

If the disruption persists, traders expect premiums to widen further across India and other Asian markets that source heavily from Dubai; India alone accounts for a substantial share of global gold demand, and its refiners and jewelers rely on a steady pipeline through Dubai.

Analysts said the situation is similar to the early days of the pandemic in 2020, when travel restrictions severed the link between London and New York and created unprecedented arbitrage opportunities.

On Wednesday, gold climbed more than 2% before paring most of the gains, helped by some weakness in the dollar after a rally earlier in the week.

"In the near term, gold remains caught between safe-haven demand and macro pressure," ING analysts said in a note. "Sustained upside would require either prolonged geopolitical stress or renewed Fed easing, while silver continues to exaggerate moves on both sides."

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for March delivery closed +0.2% to $5,120.20/oz, its third gain in four sessions, while front-month Comex March silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended -0.3% to $82.633/oz, its third consecutive daily decline.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: PMGC Holdings announces 1-for-6 reverse stock split
data.description:
• PMGC Holdings (ELAB) will effect a 1-for-6 reverse stock split of its issued and outstanding and authorized common stock, par value $0.0001 per share, the company said on Wednesday.
  • The company anticipates that there will be ~541,461 shares of common stock issued and outstanding following the anticipated reverse stock split, down from 3.25M previously. 
  • ELAB -1.90% after hours to $1.03.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kinaxis CFO to step down in May
data.description:
• Kinaxis (KXS:CA) announced that chief financial officer Blaine Fitzgerald will step down.
  • Fitzgerald will remain in his role through May 8, 2026, following the company’s first-quarter earnings call.
  • The company will immediately begin a search for a new CFO.

data.title: Yext cuts size of share tender offer to $140 million, extends deadline
data.description:
• Yext (NYSE: YEXT) on Wednesday said that it amended its modified Dutch auction tender offer to repurchase shares, reducing the maximum aggregate purchase price to $140 million from $180 million.
  • The company also extended the expiration of the tender offer to March 18, 2026, from March 12.
  • Yext said the reduction followed a reassessment of borrowing additional funds under its credit facilities due to higher capital costs amid recent macroeconomic developments.
  • The company said 3,000 shares had been tendered for purchase as of the latest update, according to information from the depositary.
  • YEXT -0.70% after hours to $5.66.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Visa, Mastercard end in the red after six-session rally
data.description:
Payment processing firms Visa (V) and Mastercard (MA) snapped six straight sessions of gains on Wednesday. The former lost 0.11% to end at $320.47, while the latter closed 0.27% lower at $522.92.

Since February 24, Visa has gained 4.43%, while Mastercard added 5.29%. The rise came after stocks of both companies declined heavily in the previous session on the back of a research report that revived fears that stablecoin-based technology will obviate the need for traditional card networks. V registered a 4.50% fall, while MA plunged 5.77%

Specific to credit card payment stocks, the Citrini Research note theorized that agentic AI turned to stablecoin-based systems to execute transactions more cheaply.

However, Seeking Alpha analyst Secret Compounders defended Mastercard, arguing that the article ignores deeply ingrained consumer habits and Mastercard's core value proposition.

"Consumers actively prefer credit cards because they view the friction as a benefit, not a problem. They love being able to easily dispute charges at the end of the month while earning cash back on every swipe. The 2-3% interchange fee is certainly a complaint for the merchants who pay it. Today, many alternative payment systems are formed because of merchant pushback,” it said.

MA’s value-added services segment, which includes cybersecurity and data monetization, is a key and often overlooked growth driver, the analyst additionally noted.

Meanwhile, Ray Merola was bullish on Visa with a Buy rating on the back of record outstanding financial and operational results.

Both companies have been rated as Buy by Seeking Alpha analysts, while Wall Street analysts have issued a Strong Buy call. As per Seeking Alpha’s quant rating, both V and MA have been rated Hold.

data.title: NRC Health says total recurring contract value exceeds $152 million
data.description:
• National Research (NRC) said on Wednesday its total recurring contract value (TRCV) exceeded $152 million as of March 4, representing a 6% increase since the end of 2025 and a 13% rise year over year.
  • The company said TRCV reflects revenue expected to be recognized over the next 12 months from renewable contracts.
  • NRC Health said the milestone does not represent its full financial results for the quarter ending March 31, 2026.
  • NRC +0.15% after hours to $13.14.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Broadcom in charts: Revenue from semiconductor solutions rises 52% Y/Y
ReGen III launches $4M private placement financing
data.description:
• ReGen III (GIII:CA) has launched a non-brokered private placement of up to 20M units at $0.20/unit.
  • The offering could generate gross proceeds of up to $4M.
  • Net proceeds will be used for working capital, general corporate purposes, and debt repayment.
  • Each unit consists of one common share and one-half of one common share purchase warrant.
  • Each whole warrant allows the holder to purchase one additional common share at $0.30 per share.
  • The warrants are exercisable for a period of three years from the date of issuance.
